2604.20279.title: AgentLens: Adaptive Visual Modalities for Human-Agent Interaction in Mobile GUI Agents
2604.20279.paper_summary: Mobile GUI agents can automate smartphone tasks by interacting directly with app interfaces, but how they should communicate with users during execution remains underexplored. Existing systems rely on two extremes: foreground execution, which maximizes transparency but prevents multitasking, and background execution, which supports multitasking but provides little visual awareness. Through iterative formative studies, we found that users prefer a hybrid model with just-in-time visual interaction, but the most effective visualization modality depends on the task. Motivated by this, we present AgentLens, a mobile GUI agent that adaptively uses three visual modalities during human-agent interaction: Full UI, Partial UI, and GenUI. AgentLens extends a standard mobile agent with adaptive communication actions and uses Virtual Display to enable background execution with selective visual overlays. In a controlled study with 21 participants, AgentLens was preferred by 85.7% of participants and achieved the highest usability (1.94 Overall PSSUQ) and adoption-intent (6.43/7).
2604.20279.paper_summary_zh: 行動 GUI 代理可以通過直接與應用程式介面互動來自動化智能手機任務，但在執行過程中它們應如何與用戶溝通仍然未被充分探索。現有系統依賴於兩種極端：前景執行，這最大化透明度但阻礙多任務處理；以及背景執行，這支持多任務處理但提供的視覺意識有限。通過迭代的形成性研究，我們發現用戶更喜歡一種具有即時視覺互動的混合模型，但最有效的視覺化模式取決於任務。受此啟發，我們提出了 AgentLens，一個在人工-代理互動中自適應使用三種視覺模式的行動 GUI 代理：完整 UI、部分 UI 和 GenUI。AgentLens 擴展了一個標準的行動代理，具有自適應的通信行為，並使用虛擬顯示來實現帶有選擇性視覺疊加的背景執行。在一項對 21 名參與者的控制研究中，85.7% 的參與者偏好 AgentLens，並達到了最高的可用性（1.94 總體 PSSUQ）和採用意圖（6.43/7）。
2604.20279.author: Jeonghyeon Kim et.al.
2604.20279.authors: Jeonghyeon Kim, Byeongjun Joung, Junwon Lee, Joohyung Lee, Taehoon Min, Sunjae Lee
